fix: replace PowerShell with bundled Node.js for Playwright install

PowerShell is not in Ubuntu's default apt repos and requires Microsoft's
package feed to install. Instead, use the Node.js binary that the Playwright
.NET NuGet package bundles in the publish output (.playwright/node/linux-x64/).

playwright.ps1 is just a thin wrapper around this same Node binary — this
change calls it directly and removes the PowerShell dependency entirely.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add crash types 27-29: ObjectAnimator, InnerClassReflection, AndroidOnClick

Type 27 - OBJECT_ANIMATOR_PROPERTY_RENAMED:
  ObjectAnimator.ofFloat(view, "propertyName") references a setter/getter
  by string. R8 renames the method → NoSuchMethodError at runtime.
  Detection: source pattern match + DEX/mapping confirmation that setter was renamed.
  Fix: -keepclassmembers with explicit setter/getter signatures.

Type 28 - INNER_CLASS_REFLECTION_RENAMED:
  Class.forName("Outer$Inner") uses $-notation for nested classes.
  R8 renames nested classes → ClassNotFoundException at runtime.
  Detection: InnerClassReflectionAnalyzer + ReflectionClassAnalyzer both detect the pattern.
  Fix: -keep class Outer { *; } + -keep class Outer$Inner { *; }

Type 29 - ANDROID_ONCLICK_METHOD_RENAMED:
  android:onClick="methodName" in layout XML resolves by reflection at runtime.
  R8 renames the handler method → NoSuchMethodException at runtime.
  Detection: AndroidOnClickAnalyzer scans layout XML for onClick attributes,
             cross-references method in source, confirms via mapping/DEX.
  Fix: -keep class ActivityOrFragment { public void handler(android.view.View); }

Changes:
- PostBuildAnalysisTask: XML scanning via resDirs, 3 new analyzer registrations,
  3 post-build confirmation branches
- ProguardProtectPlugin: populate resDirs from variant source sets
- ObjectAnimatorAnalyzer: fixed viewInheritPattern regex for constructor params
- ProguardIssue: 3 new IssueType enum entries
- HtmlReportGenerator: icons + labels for 3 new types
- proguard-rules.pro: fix rules for types 27-29 (commented out = crash mode)
- README: 29 total crash types, new type entries + example fixes

Verified: crash mode → 33 confirmed issues (types 27-29 detected)
          fix mode  → 30 confirmed issues (types 27-29 silenced, no false positives)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix game layout: min-height:0 on game-body, drop container queries for canvas

- .game-body gets min-height:0 so it can shrink inside the flex column;
  without it the default min-height:auto prevented shrinking and let
  content overflow the 100vh game-layout, pushing the map off-screen
- Removed container-type:size from .map-panel — cqh creates a layout
  cycle (element needs its own height to resolve the unit that sets it),
  causing the canvas to collapse and the map to appear ~20px tall
- New canvas sizing: height:min(100%,420px) + width:auto +
  aspect-ratio:1/1 + max-width:100%
  → height-first: fills panel height up to 420px
  → aspect-ratio derives width; max-width re-constrains if panel is
    narrower, browser resolves the square to min(H,W,420px) correctly

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: Enable autocompletion for arguments after alias commands

When users type an alias followed by a space (e.g., 'cs '), the completer
now expands the alias and provides completions for the expanded command's
arguments and subcommands.

Changes:
- Added _expand_alias_for_completion() method to resolve aliases to their
  actual command names
- Modified get_completions() to expand aliases before looking up command
  completions
- Users can now type 'cs ' and get completions for 'case show' subcommands

Example workflow:
  1. alias cs='case show'
  2. Type 'cs ' and press Tab
  3. See subcommands: show, create, run, organise, check, add, report, status

This completes the alias autocompletion feature by enabling intelligent
argument and subcommand completion for aliased commands.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Remove obsolete `XSLTHandler.startDTD` TODO comment (#439)

commit.message:
fix(ui): keep camera preview alive during minimal mode

Toggling minimal mode removed CameraPreview from the Compose tree,
which triggered surfaceDestroyed() -> stopPreview() on RootEncoder,
irreversibly stopping camera capture and killing the stream.

Fix: always keep CameraPreview in the composition tree. In minimal
mode, shrink it to 1dp (keeping the surface alive) and overlay the
MinimalStreamingOverlay on top. The encoder continues receiving
camera frames while the user sees the minimal UI.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(service): add 1 Hz stats ticker for streaming duration timer

The streaming HUD timer was always showing 00:00 because durationMs in
StreamStats was never updated. No timer coroutine existed.

Add startStatsTicker()/stopStatsTicker() to StreamingService:
- Captures stream start time in onConnectionSuccess()
- Launches a coroutine that updates _streamStats.durationMs every 1s
- Cancels the ticker in cleanupAndStop()
- Resets stats to fresh StreamStats() on new startStream()

fix(ci): treat cancelled jobs as failures in merge queue status check (#3972)

* fix(ci): treat cancelled jobs as failures in merge queue status check

The summarize-workflow action treated `cancelled` as an acceptable
result alongside `success` and `skipped`. This allowed the CI status
check to pass when upstream jobs (e.g. rate-limit-gate) were cancelled,
causing all downstream jobs to be  letting PRs through theskipped
merge queue without any checks actually running.

Remove `cancelled` from the pass-through case so it falls through to
the failure path, aligning the code with the README documentation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(kanban): animate cross-column card moves (#96)

## Summary
- normalize desktop kanban state into a shared scene model and pass it
into the UI board
- move kanban layout and cross-column motion ownership into @acepe/ui
with a board-level overlay and ghost card mode
- replace brittle source-text assertions with behavioral desktop tests
and pure UI motion/layout coverage

## Validation
- cd packages/website && bun run i18n:generate && bun run check
- cd packages/desktop && bun run check && bun test
src/lib/components/main-app-view/components/content/desktop-kanban-scene.test.ts
src/lib/components/main-app-view/components/content/kanban-view.test.ts
- cd packages/ui && bun x vitest run
src/components/kanban/kanban-board-layout.test.ts
src/components/kanban/__tests__/kanban-board-motion.test.ts

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Animate cross-column card moves in the kanban board with a board-level
overlay and ghost cards for smooth, accessible transitions. The desktop
kanban is now normalized into a shared scene model, with layout and
motion handled in `@acepe/ui`.

- **New Features**
- Smooth travel/settle animations for cross-column moves; respects
reduced motion.
- `KanbanSceneBoard` accepts a normalized `model` (columns, cards,
placements) for deterministic ordering and motion.
- Overlay-based ghost rendering prevents layout jumps during
transitions.

- **Refactors**
- Desktop builds a `KanbanSceneModel` via `buildDesktopKanbanScene`; UI
owns layout (`kanban-board-layout`) and motion (`kanban-board-motion*`)
logic.
- Replaced brittle source-text assertions with behavioral tests in
desktop and `@acepe/ui`.
- Internal board API uses per-column `layout`; `KanbanCard` adds a ghost
presentation mode.

<sup>Written for commit cb7a07991fc5f56c7efb0863ada25864fccca23b.
Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

commit.message:
Android perf: setPixelRatio, rAF-gate pointermove, anchor guard

- scrib_renderer.ts: renderer.setPixelRatio(Math.min(devicePixelRatio, 2))
  Caps the WebGL framebuffer at 2× CSS pixels. On POCO 5 (DPR ≈ 2.75) the
  previous DPR=1 framebuffer was upscaled blurrily by the OS compositor;
  DPR=2 is sharp and roughly 2× cheaper than native resolution.

- main_app.ts: rAF-gate pointermove handler
  The event handler now stashes the latest PointerEvent and schedules one
  requestAnimationFrame. The rAF callback drains the event using
  editor.pointermove() (which already uses getCoalescedEvents() for
  stroke fidelity). At 120 Hz touch rate this reduces main-thread
  pixel-work scheduling from 120×/frame to 1×/frame.

- main_app.ts: _redraw_anchor_canvas early-return when no anchors
  Previously, every pointermove triggered a full getImageData + putImageData
  on the doc-sized anchor canvas even when no anchors existed. Now the
  function returns immediately if anchor_manager.anchors.length === 0.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: cache lazy icon wrappers and add local Suspense boundary

Icon.tsx: introduce a module-level Map to cache lazy() wrappers by icon name. Each lazy() call creates a fresh wrapper with its own pending state, causing Suspense to flash on every new instance. Caching ensures a loaded icon is synchronous on subsequent renders.

ScopedPage.tsx: wrap Dynamic with a local <Suspense fallback={null}> so lazy sub-resources resolve within the page without re-triggering the global <PageLoading /> fallback.

commit.message:
Mobile performance: viewport meta, camera cache, setSize, coalesced events, narrow getImageData

1. index.html: add <meta name=viewport> — prevents 980px virtual viewport
   on Android; fixes layout, touch coords, and default zoom behaviour.

2. scrib_renderer.ts: cache OrthographicCamera — previously init_camera()
   allocated a new camera object every RAF call. Now one camera is created
   at startup; update_camera() mutates its frustum + calls updateProjectionMatrix()
   and renderer.setSize() so Three.js stays in sync with canvas resizes.

3. editor.ts: use getCoalescedEvents() in pointermove — Android fires
   60–120 touch samples between frames; all were processed sequentially per
   event, blowing the frame budget. Now all coalesced positions are fed to
   tool.drag() for correct stroke coverage, but rendering stays throttled
   to one RAF.

4. scribble.ts: narrow getImageData to stroke segment bbox — previously the
   full tool canvas (= full document) was read and written on every pointer
   move. Now only a tight bounding box (segment endpoints ± radius + 1px)
   is transferred. For a 500px document this reduces the pixel transfer from
   250k to ~(2r+3)² per event (~hundreds vs millions of bytes).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Complete plan-51: Optimize ECS Express deployment to the minimum safe footprint to reduce cost

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add EZTest.exe native Windows launcher

Compiled C# WinForms application that starts the EZTest wizard
silently and opens http://localhost:7433 in the default browser.
No terminal window, no VBScript dependency (fixed Windows 11 24H2
where VBScript is disabled by default).

- launcher/EZTestLauncher.cs: C# source with auto-root detection,
  first-time build prompt, hidden node process, browser open
- launcher/build.ps1: rebuilds EZTest.exe using .NET Framework
  csc.exe (no SDK required) — run via: npm run build:launcher
- package.json: added build:launcher script
- .gitignore: removed EZTest.exe exclusion (committed for convenience)
- CHANGELOG.md: documented launcher addition

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: allow API key auth on VenuesController

VenuesController only accepted MultiAuth (JWT) scheme. Added ApiKey
to the auth schemes so the from-url endpoint (and all venue endpoints)
work with X-API-Key header for iOS Shortcuts and other integrations.
